commits.subject: fix(icon): convert dm lettermark from text to outlined paths
commits.body:
SVG <text> elements depend on fonts being available at render time.
Sharp rasterizes server-side and may not have Arial Black installed,
causing fallback rendering or blank output. Replaced with hand-drawn
filled paths that render identically in all environments.

Fixed "d" bowl direction (stem on right, bowl opens left) — previous
path version read as "bm" because the stem was on the left like a "b".

commits.subject: fix(ci): update Node to 22, fix audit vulnerability, update description
commits.body:
- CI Node version: 20 -> 22. Vite 7.3.1 requires ^20.19.0 || >=22.12.0.
  Node 20 in CI may resolve to a version below 20.19.0, breaking the
  build. Node 22 matches local dev environment.
- npm audit fix: resolved high-severity picomatch vulnerability
  (transitive dep of Vite, build-time only, not exploitable at runtime
  but keeps CI clean)
- Updated package.json description to match current project purpose

Tags: ci, deps, security

commits.subject: fix(security): escape all dynamic HTML content, normalize URLs
commits.body:
Security:
- All meta.json values rendered into HTML are now escaped via
  escapeHtml() — badge, repo, privacy labels/values, use cases,
  liveUrl, repoUrl, and markdown table headers. Prevents XSS if
  meta.json ever contains user-controlled or malicious content.
- escapeHtml already handles &, <, >, " (added in previous commit)

Consistency:
- Normalized all Vercel URLs to include trailing slash — few-lap,
  synctone, sun-sea-o were missing it in both index.html and
  meta.json. All 12 live URLs now consistently end with /

Tags: security, xss, urls, cleanup

Risk: low
Debt: paid

commits.subject: fix(bugs): JS bugs, visual issues, focus states, print improvements
commits.body:
Bugs fixed:
- Event listener accumulation: Tab click handlers were added inside
  render(), accumulating on every re-render. Switched to event
  delegation on document (one listener handles all tabs)
- escapeHtml missing quote escaping: Added " — prevents broken
  HTML attributes if meta.json contains quotes
- Copy button selector too broad: querySelector('.doc-action-btn')
  always selected the first button. Now uses getElementById('copy-btn')

Visual fixes:
- Status dot colors: Hardcoded #22c55e/#a1a1aa replaced with
  var(--pico-primary)/var(--pico-muted-color) for dark mode
- Logo border-radius: Hardcoded 6px replaced with
  var(--pico-border-radius) for consistency
- Logo href: Dead anchor (#) replaced with ./ (home link)

Accessibility:
- Added :focus-visible outlines to card-links, doc-tabs, and
  doc-action-btn — keyboard navigation now shows focus indicators

Print:
- Added break-inside: avoid to table and pre elements — prevents
  code blocks and tables from splitting across pages

Tags: fix, bugs, accessibility, visual, print

Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid

commits.subject: fix(mobile): touch targets, safe area insets, grid overflow, active states
commits.body:
Mobile UX fixes identified during user/design perspective review:

- Touch targets: Icon buttons increased from 36px to 44px (2.75rem),
  card links get padding for 44px+ tap area, doc tabs and action
  buttons get min-height: 2.75rem — all meet Apple HIG 44px minimum
- Safe area insets: Added viewport-fit=cover to both pages and
  env(safe-area-inset-*) to body, nav, and footer — prevents content
  overlap with iPhone notch/Dynamic Island
- Grid overflow: Lowered card-grid minmax from 300px to 280px and
  card-grid-sm from 260px to 250px — prevents 2-column overflow on
  576-640px screens where Pico's container leaves ~560px available
- Active states: Added :active opacity to icon buttons, card links,
  article cards, and doc action buttons — provides touch feedback
  since :hover doesn't fire on mobile
- Dynamic meta description: project.html now sets <meta description>
  from meta.json after load — improves SEO when sharing project links

Tags: mobile, ux, touch, accessibility, seo
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: portfolio-site
Semver: patch

commits.body:

elements. Using <nav> for card action links (Details, Live app,
Source) incorrectly received Pico's nav styling. Changed to <div>
since these are action links within cards, not site navigation.

- Removed section margin override (let Pico handle spacing)
- Removed redundant hero/print padding overrides
- Added !important to .icon-btn overrides (Pico uses high-specificity
  selectors on <button> elements, setting width: 100% by default)
- Merged duplicate .card-grid > article selectors
- Documented why card-links must be <div> not <nav>

commits.subject: feat(design): integrate Pico CSS Jade theme with Space Grotesk + Inter fonts
commits.body:
Complete restyle of the portfolio site:

- Pico CSS v2 Jade theme imported via Vite from node_modules (not CDN)
  for consistent build pipeline and no external runtime dependency
- Space Grotesk (headings) + Inter (body) via Google Fonts with preconnect
- Switched dark mode from .dark class to data-theme attribute — Pico uses
  [data-theme="dark"] for its dark mode selectors
- Updated flash prevention inline scripts on both pages
- Updated theme.js toggle, cross-tab sync, and OS preference tracking
- Restructured HTML to Pico's semantic conventions: <nav> for header,
  <article> for cards with <header>/<footer>, proper list structure
- Rewrote styles.css as override layer — removed all base typography,
  colors, focus states (Pico handles these). Only card grids, badges,
  icon buttons, and print overrides remain as custom CSS
- Added color-scheme meta tag for browser chrome

Tags: design, pico-css, fonts, dark-mode, restyle

Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing

commits.subject: fix(edge-cases): burger menu action throw, cross-tab storage removal
commits.body:
Two edge case fixes found during parallel agent verification:

1. BurgerMenu: if item.action() throws, onClose() was never called,
   leaving the menu stuck open. Now wraps action in try/catch and
   always calls onClose() regardless of success.

2. useDarkMode cross-tab sync: when another tab removes the darkMode
   key from localStorage (e.newValue === null), the handler silently
   ignored it, leaving this tab with a stale preference. Now falls
   back to system preference via matchMedia, matching the hook's
   fresh-load behavior.

Tags: edge-case, dark-mode, burger-menu, resilience

Risk: low
Debt: paid

commits.subject: feat(menu): extract BurgerMenu.vue with a11y hardening + z-index scale
commits.body:
Extracted burger menu from AppLayout into standalone BurgerMenu.vue with
all glow-props disclosure pattern fixes. Formalized z-index scale in
CLAUDE.md and normalized all components to match.

Burger Menu Hardening:
- Disclosure pattern: aria-expanded, aria-controls, <nav>+<ul>/<li>
  (not role="menu" — wrong ARIA semantics for navigation)
- Escape key handler (registered only while open)
- cursor-pointer on backdrop (iOS Safari empty div fix)
- overscroll-contain on dropdown (prevents scroll chaining)
- Focus management: first item on open, trigger on close
  (hasBeenOpen guard prevents focus steal on mount)
- focus-visible:ring-2 on items for keyboard navigation
- AppLayout simplified: items as computed data, BurgerMenu handles UX

Z-Index Scale (CLAUDE.md):
- Added scale table (0→80 range)
- Debug pill: z-[9999] → z-[80]
- Toast: z-[100] → z-[70]

CLAUDE.md Sync:
- Added "Discontinued repos" AI note (plant-fur, coin-zapp)

Tags: a11y, ux, burger-menu, z-index, glow-props-sync, refactor
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: glow-props-sync

commits.subject: feat(theme): implement full dark mode system
commits.body:
User-controlled dark/light mode with system preference fallback,
localStorage persistence, cross-tab sync, and flash prevention.
Follows glow-props CLAUDE.md "Theme & Dark Mode" pattern adapted
to Vue 3 Composition API.

Foundation:
- Semantic CSS variable palette (:root light + .dark dark tokens)
- @custom-variant dark for Tailwind v4 class-based toggling
- color-scheme: dark on html.dark for native form inputs
- useDarkMode.ts composable (singleton, localStorage, matchMedia,
  cross-tab sync via storage event, dynamic meta theme-color)
- Flash prevention inline script in index.html <head>
- Dual <meta name="theme-color"> tags with media queries
- @media print overrides (no-print class, white bg, break-inside)

UI:
- Dark/Light mode toggle in burger menu with Sun/Moon icons
- dark: variants on 30 files (17 components + 8 views + CSS + HTML)
- ApexCharts theme-aware config (axis labels, grid, tooltip, legend)
- HelpDrawer prose styles use semantic CSS variables

Tags: theme, dark-mode, ux, glow-props-sync, css, composable
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: neutral
Epic: dark-mode
Semver: minor

commits.subject: feat(ui): z-index scale, burger menu disclosure pattern, timer audit
commits.body:
Three glow-props alignment items:

1. Z-index scale standardization: Aligned all 21 z-index values
   across 11 files to canonical scale — canvas 0-10, headers 20,
   sheets 30, nav 40, menu 40/50, modals 60, toasts 70, debug 80.

2. Burger menu disclosure pattern: Extracted BurgerMenu.jsx from
   inline MobileLayout code. Adds cursor-pointer on backdrop (iOS
   Safari fix), hasBeenOpenRef focus guard, overscroll-contain,
   useId() for aria-controls, Escape key handler, nav+ul/li
   disclosure semantics (replaces incorrect role=menu/menuitem).

3. Timer leak audit: All 9 useEffect hooks with setTimeout/setInterval
   have proper cleanup. No nested timeouts found. No changes needed.

commits.subject: feat(dark-mode): cross-tab sync, meta theme-color, color-scheme, safe storage
commits.body:
Implement all 4 high-priority glow-props alignment items for dark mode:

1. Cross-tab sync: storage event listener updates all open tabs when
   dark mode is toggled in any tab.
2. Dynamic meta theme-color: replaced static #7c3aed with two
   media-queried tags (light=#FAFAFA, dark=#0F0F23). Hook updates
   both via querySelectorAll on manual toggle.
3. color-scheme: dark on html.dark: native form inputs, selects,
   and scrollbars now match dark theme.
4. Safe localStorage: wrapped all calls in safeStorageGet/safeStorageSet
   with try/catch to prevent crashes in sandboxed iframes.

Bonus: added dark mode flash prevention inline script in index.html
to apply .dark class from localStorage before first paint.

Tags: dark-mode, pwa, a11y, resilience, glow-props-alignment

commits.subject: fix(cache): add cache-busting to sample manifest fetch
commits.body:
The previous NetworkFirst SW fix wasn't enough because jsDelivr CDN
caches @main URLs for ~24 hours at the edge, and browsers can serve
stale HTTP-cached responses before the SW even sees the request.

Appends an hourly-rotating ?v= param to the manifest fetch URL,
bypassing both jsDelivr edge cache and browser HTTP cache. Also
updates the SW regex to match manifest URLs with query strings.

Tags: pwa, cache, media, sample-images
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: neutral

Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix(pwa): use NetworkFirst for sample images manifest
commits.body:
StaleWhileRevalidate always serves the stale cached manifest first,
requiring 3 app opens before new images appear. The manifest is ~3KB
so the network cost is negligible. NetworkFirst with a 5s timeout
ensures users see fresh images on first load after SW update, with
offline fallback.

Tags: pwa, cache, service-worker, sample-images
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: sample-images
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01D8KRFu6iV5xhR34PXfS1Pj
commits.title: use NetworkFirst for sample images manifest

commits.subject: fix(pwa): service worker regex missing @main in CDN URL pattern
commits.body:
The manifest.json URL contains @main (canva-grid-assets@main/manifest.json)
but the SW urlPattern regex expected a bare slash after the repo name. This
caused the manifest to fall through to the CacheFirst images rule instead of
StaleWhileRevalidate, locking users into stale manifests for up to 30 days.

Add optional (@[^/]+)? group to both CDN URL patterns so they match
versioned jsDelivr URLs (@main, @v2, etc.).

Tags: pwa, cache, service-worker, sample-images
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid

commits.subject: docs(claude-md): add Theme & Dark Mode to Suggested Implementations
commits.body:
Add comprehensive Theme & Dark Mode pattern covering:
- CSS variable palette with semantic tokens (raw hex, v3/v4 compatible)
- Tailwind dark mode config for both v3 (darkMode: 'class') and v4
  (@custom-variant dark)
- useDarkMode web hook with localStorage persistence, system preference
  fallback, safe storage access (try/catch for sandboxed iframes)
- Flash prevention inline script in index.html (same early-capture
  pattern as PWA beforeinstallprompt)
- Dual meta theme-color tags with media queries
- React Native useAppTheme context with AsyncStorage persistence
- themeHydrated flag to prevent splash screen hiding before stored
  preference loads (fixes flash bug found in few-lap audit)
- color-scheme: dark on html.dark for native form elements
- Print override forcing light colors
- Related patterns: currentColor SVGs, chart theme-awareness,
  content themes vs app dark mode distinction
- 13 Key Lessons covering all edge cases and gotchas

Findings from double-check audit:
- localStorage needs try/catch (SecurityError in sandboxed iframes)
- theme() function deprecated in Tailwind v4 (use raw hex)
- textTertiary contrast values were swapped (fixed to pass WCAG AA)
- AsyncStorage hydration flash in React Native (hold splash screen)
- No CSS transitions on switch (industry standard: instant)
- CSP inline script caveat documented
- Android PWA manifest theme_color override documented

Tags: documentation, ui, dark-mode, accessibility, cross-project
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: neutral

commits.subject: fix(platforms): correct 3 platform dimensions verified against current specs
commits.body:
Web-searched all 13 new platform dimensions against official/authoritative
sources. 10 were correct, 3 needed updating:

1. YouTube End Screen: 1280×720 → 1920×1080
   Was using thumbnail size, not full end screen template size.
   Source: Snappa/Riverside YouTube outro guides (2026)

2. Product Square: 1000×1000 → 2000×2000
   1000px was Amazon's minimum. Modern e-commerce platforms (Shopify,
   Amazon, eBay) all recommend 2000px+ for zoom functionality.
   Source: Shopify/Squareshot product image guides (2026)

3. Product Portrait: 800×1200 → 1200×1800
   Too small for modern displays. Increased to match 2:3 ratio at
   a size that supports zoom on high-DPI screens.
   Source: Photoroom/Shopify image size guides (2026)

commits.subject: fix(security): add sandbox attribute to script-embed iframe in embed.js
commits.body:
The EmbedCodeDialog iframe snippet was sandboxed in the previous commit,
but the script embed path (embed.js) creates iframes dynamically without
sandbox. This adds the same sandbox restrictions to the recommended embed
method for consistent security across both embed paths.

sandbox="allow-same-origin allow-scripts allow-popups allow-forms"

Verified: postMessage auto-resize, isEmbedMode() detection, Supabase
auth cookies, and form JavaScript all work within these sandbox rules.

Tags: security, embed

commits.body:
High Priority (3 items):

1. Unassigned image feedback: addImage() returns { id, assigned } instead

   violation (silent failure on user action).

2. Accessibility pass: ~20 elements across 5 files — aria-label on
   platform chevrons/format buttons (PlatformPreview), role="menuitem"
   on mobile menu items (App), aria-label on overlay type buttons
   (StyleTab), aria-label on color picker (TemplatesTab),
   aria-hidden="true" on decorative SVGs (MobileNav, App),
   aria-label="Main navigation" on nav (MobileNav).

3. Phase 4 platform format data: 15 new formats across 7 groups —
   Pinterest (Pin, Story), Snapchat (Ad, Story), YouTube (Thumbnail,
   End Screen), WhatsApp (Status), Threads (Post, Story), Product
   Images (Square, Portrait), Store Banners (Hero, Category). New
   'ecommerce' category. Total: 18 groups, 42 formats.

Medium Priority (3 items):

4. Lazy font loading: Only 2 active fonts load on mount via fontsToLoad
   useMemo. Remaining 13 load when StyleTab Fonts section expands
   (CollapsibleSection onExpand callback). Reduces initial requests
   from 15 to 2.

5. Looks per-element text styling: Added textStyles to all 20 look
   presets with per-element color/bold overrides (5 palettes: clean,
   bold, dark, warm, artistic). applyStylePreset merges into text
   elements preserving content/visible/size.

6. Calculate imageAspectRatio: Derived from first image naturalWidth/
   Height in App.jsx, passed to TemplatesTab. Suggested layouts now
   adapt to image orientation instead of returning fixed 3 defaults.

commits.subject: fix(modules): resolve 10 issues in contract upload feature
commits.body:
Bugs fixed:
- Stale props: ContractUploadPreview ignored new candidates when
  sub-section toggle changed. Added key prop to force remount.
- Duplicate flow infinite loop: "Upload anyway" re-opened file picker
  which re-triggered duplicate detection. Now stores ParseResult in
  duplicate state and proceeds directly to preview.
- Retry progress tracking: retrying failed modules replaced the entire
  progress array with a shorter one, losing successful items. Now merges
  retry results back at the correct indices.
- hardSplitAtWordBoundary title count: used pre-filter chunk count in
  titles like "(2/5)" when only 3 chunks existed. Now filters first.
- handleSplitOversized double computation: called splitOversizedSection
  twice. Now computes once and reuses.

Standards fixes:
- Touch targets: preview move buttons were 36px, below WCAG 2.5.5
  minimum 44px. Matched to existing ModuleCard pattern.
- Duplicated MODULE_CONTENT_LIMIT: exported from contractSplitter,
  imported in ContractUploadPreview instead of redeclaring.
- BATCH_SIZE inside component: moved to module scope as UPLOAD_BATCH_SIZE.
- PDF type annotation: replaced complex conditional type inference with
  simple let + await pattern, letting TypeScript infer the type.
- recordUpload on partial failure: hash was recorded even when some
  modules failed, causing false duplicate warnings on retry. Now only
  records on full success.

commits.subject: feat(modules): add contract upload and conversion to modules
commits.body:
Upload traditional contracts (.txt, .docx, .pdf) and convert them into
Sancio's module-based structure. Client-side extraction with no backend
changes needed.

Key design decisions:
- PDF library (pdfjs-dist) lazy-loaded via dynamic import with Vite-bundled
  worker — zero cost for non-PDF users, works offline (no CDN dependency)
- Heuristic section splitting at depth 0 only by default to avoid module
  proliferation; sub-section splitting is opt-in
- Batch creation in parallel groups of 4 via Promise.allSettled to handle
  SA mobile latency on 30+ module uploads
- Source tracking via [Source: filename] note convention — syncs to both
  parties via Supabase, searchable, no schema changes needed
- Duplicate detection via text hash in localStorage
- Encoding detection: BOM → UTF-8 strict → Windows-1252 fallback
- Oversized sections (>10k chars) split via paragraph → sentence →
  word-boundary fallback chain

New files:
- src/utils/documentParser.ts — text extraction for .txt/.docx/.pdf
- src/utils/contractSplitter.ts — heuristic section splitting
- src/components/modules/ContractUploadPreview.tsx — preview/edit UI

Modified files:
- src/components/modules/TemplateSelector.tsx — tabbed UI with upload tab
- src/pages/AgreementDetailPage.tsx — batch creation handler

Tags: feature, modules, upload, parsing, pdf, docx

commits.subject: fix(audit): address 5 code audit findings — race conditions, data safety, defensive coding
commits.body:
1. ExportButtons: Add ref-based mutex (exportLockRef) to prevent concurrent
   exports. React state batching delays the disabled prop, allowing rapid
   clicks to fire two handlers that both modify canvas transform/opacity.
   Lock is checked synchronously at handler entry, released in finally.

2. useAdState loadDesign: Filter page data to PAGE_FIELDS before spreading
   into state. Saved designs from older versions may contain orphaned fields
   that would permanently pollute state if spread without filtering.

3. useAdState extractPageData: Wrap structuredClone in try/catch with JSON
   round-trip fallback. Prevents page swap crashes if non-cloneable data
   (DOM refs, Blobs) leaks into state.

4. cellUtils cleanupOrphanedCells: Add NaN guard to parseInt and extract
   shared pruneOrphanedKeys helper. Non-numeric keys from corrupted state
   silently survived cleanup across all 5 cell-indexed data structures.

5. debugLog: Guard global error/unhandledrejection listeners with window
   flag to prevent accumulation during Vite HMR reloads.

Tags: audit, race-condition, data-safety, defensive-coding, hmr
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: code-review-sweep
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix(review): address 12 code review findings across 10 components
commits.body:
Fixes bugs, UX issues, and code quality problems found during review sweep:

1. MediaTab: Replace mutable cellIndex in render with useMemo+Map pattern
   (prevents StrictMode double-render bugs)
2. BottomSheet: Add onTouchCancel handler to prevent sheet freezing when
   touch is interrupted by system UI
3. ExportButtons: Replace fixed setTimeout(300) in PDF multi-page export
   with double waitForPaint() for reliability on slow devices
4. ContextBar: Improve delete confirmation label from "Delete p1?" to
   "Delete page 1?" for clarity
5. LogoUploader: Add 10MB file size validation before DataURL conversion
   to prevent browser freezes on large files
6. SaveLoadModal: Include time in default design name for uniqueness when
   saving multiple designs on the same day
7. MobileNav: Show active tab indicator even when bottom sheet is closed
   so users know which tab will open on next tap
8. PlatformPreview: Reset showTips state when platform changes to prevent
   displaying stale tips from previous platform
9. StyleTab: Extract repeated overlay type button into OverlayTypeButton
   component, reducing ~50 lines of duplication
10. TemplatesTab: Make empty layout filter message actionable with
    suggestion to change shape or category
11. SaveLoadModal: Add aria-label to search input for screen readers

commits.subject: feat(ui): add responsive pagination across all list sections
commits.body:
Create useShowMore hook for consistent "Show more" pagination with
different limits for mobile vs desktop. Apply to 7 locations:

- DetailPane: 10 mobile / 20 desktop (was unbounded — could freeze)
- Timeline commits: 10 mobile / 25 desktop (was 100 — too many)
- Progress epics: 6 mobile / 12 desktop (was unbounded)
- Contributors: 6 mobile / 8 desktop (was hardcoded 8)
- Tags: 8 mobile / all desktop (was unbounded)
- Discover files: 5 mobile / 10 desktop (was hardcoded 10)
- Projects: 6 mobile / 12 desktop (was unbounded)

Each section shows a "Show more" button that loads the next batch.
Limits are calibrated to avoid overwhelming scroll on mobile while
considering co-located sections sharing the same tab.

commits.body:
Without allow-same-origin, sandboxed iframes are treated as opaque origins
and cannot make fetch/XHR requests to load chart data from the repo-tor API.
The embed script loaded successfully but charts rendered empty because all
network requests from within the iframe were blocked.

commits.subject: fix(audit): unmount guards, async error handling, stale run cancellation
commits.body:
- Add mountedRef guard to InterestForm so diagnoseFailure state updates
  are skipped after unmount (prevents React warnings and memory leaks)
- Make UpdatePrompt.handleUpdate async to catch rejected promises from
  onUpdate (previously only caught synchronous exceptions)
- Remove needRefresh from SW polling interval dependency — polling should
  run once from mount, not restart on every update state change. Read
  registrationRef dynamically inside interval to handle late registration.
- Add monotonic run counter to DebugBanner runDiagnostics to cancel stale
  diagnostic runs when a new run starts (prevents interleaved state updates)

Tags: bugfix, race-condition, async, unmount, polling
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: internal
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: code-quality
Semver: patch

  Referrer-Policy, and Permissions-Policy

  embedded content from accessing parent cookies/localStorage

  exposing the endpoint in source control

commits.subject: fix(pwa): fix 3 bugs found in code review
commits.body:
1. usePWAInstall: consumeEarlyCapturedEvent() race condition — was called
   inside useState initializer, meaning multiple component mounts would race
   to delete the same window property (second mount gets null). Fixed by
   moving consumption to module level (runs once at import time).

2. usePWAUpdate: offlineReady auto-dismiss missing notifyListeners() — when
   the 3-second timer cleared _offlineReady, sibling components subscribed
   to the module-level listener were never notified of the state change.

3. InstallInstructionsModal: 'unknown' browser type fell through to Chrome
   desktop instructions ("Click the install icon in the address bar") which
   is incorrect for unrecognized browsers. Added explicit generic fallback.

Tags: bugfix, pwa, code-review
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: claude-md-alignment
Semver: patch

commits.subject: refactor(pwa,debug): align usePWAInstall, usePWAUpdate, and debugLog with glow-props patterns
commits.body:
Align three implemented patterns with the shared glow-props reference:

**usePWAInstall.ts:**
- Granular browser detection: 'chrome'|'edge'|'brave'|'safari-ios'|'safari-macos'|
  'firefox-android'|'firefox-desktop'|'unknown' (was 4 types)
- Add dismissed state persistence in localStorage (prevents install UI from
  reappearing after user dismisses)
- Add consumeEarlyCapturedEvent() to read from +html.tsx inline script capture
- Add localStorage-based analytics tracking (last 50 events for debugging)
- Export BrowserType, needsManualInstructions, and dismiss callback
- Preserve existing canInstall, shouldShowInstall, install API

**+html.tsx:**
- Add inline beforeinstallprompt capture script (earliest possible capture,
  runs during HTML parse before any JS modules load)

**InstallInstructionsModal.tsx:**
- Update to handle granular BrowserType with platform-specific browser names
  (e.g., "Install via Edge", "Install on Brave (Android)")

**usePWAUpdate.ts:**
- Add offlineReady state with 3-second auto-dismiss
- Detects when SW is active + controlling + no pending updates

**debugLog.ts:**
- Add typed DebugSource union with string fallback for autocomplete
- Add milliseconds to timestamp format (HH:MM:SS.mmm)
- Add getDebugLogCount() helper
- Add automatic global error/unhandledrejection listeners at module load (dev only)

**PWADebugBanner.tsx:**
- Update to check both new and legacy window property names

Tags: pwa, debug, alignment, glow-props, code-quality
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: claude-md-alignment
Semver: minor

commits.subject: fix(lint): resolve all ESLint errors and harden PWA manifest
commits.body:
Three pre-existing lint issues fixed:

1. usePWAInstall.ts — setState called synchronously in useEffect.
   Moved early beforeinstallprompt consumption to module-level variable
   so both useRef initializer and useState initializer read it without
   triggering cascading renders or reading refs during render.

2. DebugBanner.tsx — useCallback missing canInstall dependency.
   Added canInstall to the dependency array so diagnostics re-run
   when install state changes.

3. vite.config.ts — PWA manifest missing id and prefer_related_applications.
   Added id: '/' for stable app identity across redeployments and
   prefer_related_applications: false to prevent Chrome from suppressing
   the beforeinstallprompt event.

ESLint now passes with 0 errors, 0 warnings.

Tags: lint, pwa, hooks, code-quality

commits.subject: fix(data): add HTTPS proxy support to geocode-all script
commits.body:
Node.js https.get does not respect the https_proxy/HTTPS_PROXY env var
(unlike curl). In proxy-only environments (CI containers, sandboxed
hosts), direct TCP connections fail with DNS errors while curl succeeds
through the proxy tunnel.

Added HTTP CONNECT tunneling: when https_proxy is set, the script
establishes a CONNECT tunnel through the proxy, then makes HTTPS
requests through the tunneled socket. Falls back to direct connections
when no proxy is configured.

commits.subject: feat(data): geocode all stations — no import without country validation
commits.body:
Previously the pipeline split stations into two paths: "accepted" (had
address+city from OSM tags, auto-labeled ZA) and "skipped" (needed
geocoding). This meant ~95% of border-zone stations without addr:country
tags could enter the import as ZA without any validation.

New approach: every station gets geocoded via Nominatim.
- Transform (step 2) now outputs all elements for geocoding, only
  rejecting those explicitly tagged as non-SA
- geocode-all.mjs (step 3) validates country_code for every station
  and merges data: OSM for street-level detail, Nominatim for admin
  hierarchy (city, suburb, province, country)
- Province coverage jumps from ~40% to ~100%
- Expected runtime: ~70 min for ~4,200 stations (resumable via cache)

Merge strategy tested with mock data — OSM street address wins,
Nominatim admin hierarchy wins, OSM postal code wins with Nominatim
fallback.

Tags: data, osm, geocoding, data-integrity
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 3
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: data-extraction
Semver: minor

https://claude.ai/code/session_012GoRRuSkXvRWbQhj6XTrxn
commits.title: geocode all stations — no import without country validation

commits.subject: fix(data): add 3-layer country filter with border zone detection
commits.body:
The addr:country tag only covers ~5% of stations near borders (tested
on 110 elements in Eswatini border zone). Untagged stations with valid
address/city were being auto-accepted as ZA — a confirmed data integrity
bug.

Three-layer filter:
1. addr:country tag rejects non-ZA when present
2. Border zone detection routes untagged near-border stations to reverse
   geocoding instead of auto-accepting
3. Nominatim reverse geocode validates country_code === 'za'

Tested: Eswatini border zone — 5 accepted (tagged ZA), 104 routed to
geocode (untagged), 0 SA stations wrongly excluded.

Tags: data, osm, country-filter, testing
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: data-extraction
Semver: patch

commits.title: add 3-layer country filter with border zone detection

commits.subject: fix(data): replace area filter with bbox + post-filter for reliability
commits.body:
The Overpass area filter area["ISO3166-1"="ZA"] was untested and carries
risks: slower polygon queries, higher timeout risk with way/relation
elements, and temporary unavailability if the ZA boundary relation is
being edited.

Switched to the proven bbox approach with a two-layer country filter:
1. Transform rejects stations with addr:country != ZA
2. Reverse geocode validates country_code === 'za' from Nominatim

This is faster, more reliable, and can be switched to area filter later
if bbox leakage proves significant.

Tags: data, osm, reliability

Impact: infrastructure

commits.subject: docs(data): add known gaps analysis to extraction pipeline
commits.body:
Identified 9 gaps in the current pipeline after reviewing the transform
script and RPC function:

1. Missing way/relation OSM elements (only fetching nodes)
2. No dedup — RPC does plain INSERT, re-runs create duplicates
3. No update path for changed station data on re-runs
4. No deactivation of stations removed from OSM
5. Bounding box leaks neighboring country stations
6. Province abbreviations (GP, WC, etc.) not mapped
7. Batch file format doesn't match RPC expected input
8. Reverse geocode script not yet implemented
9. No validation of geocode results

Added prioritized gap table, fixes for each, and updated TODO.md
with specific action items cross-referenced to gap numbers.

Impact: internal
Risk: low

commits.subject: feat(data): add Overpass API transform script for OSM fuel station import
commits.body:
Adds a Node.js script that transforms raw Overpass API data (OpenStreetMap
fuel stations in South Africa) into the bulk-import JSON format accepted by
the bulk_insert_stations RPC function.

The Overpass API provides 4,215 fuel stations for SA in a single query.
The transform maps OSM tags to our station schema: brand (85%), phone (63%),
amenities, operating hours, loyalty programs, payment methods, and province.

2,733 stations pass validation with 0 errors. The remaining 1,482 lack
address/city data in OSM and would need reverse geocoding to fill in.

Output is written as batched JSON files (1000 per batch) for safe import
via the admin bulk-import UI.

Usage:
  curl -sL --max-time 120 -X POST \
    -d 'data=[out:json][timeout:100];node[amenity=fuel](-35,16,-22,33);out body;' \
    "https://overpass-api.de/api/interpreter" -o data/overpass-raw.json
  node scripts/transform-overpass.mjs

Tags: data, osm, overpass, bulk-import, extraction
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: infrastructure
Risk: low
Debt: neutral
Epic: data-extraction

commits.subject: fix(mobile): eliminate bottom sheet lag by using GPU-composited transforms
commits.body:
The bottom sheet was animating CSS `height`, which triggers layout
recalculation (reflow) on every frame. During drag, every touchmove
called setState in App.jsx, re-rendering the entire component tree.

Fix: Sheet now uses a fixed height with `transform: translateY()` for
show/hide animation (GPU-composited, no layout reflow). During drag,
the DOM is updated directly via refs — React state only updates on
snap (touchend), eliminating mid-drag re-renders.

Tags: mobile, ui, performance, bottom-sheet
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: mobile-ux
Semver: patch
commits.title: eliminate bottom sheet lag by using GPU-composited transforms

Sancio endpoints to snake_case, matching the existing response convention
(raw Supabase rows). This eliminates the naming inconsistency where
validated fields (via validation.ts) used snake_case but inline-validated
fields used camelCase.

Fields renamed:
- projectId → project_id (invite, verify-passphrase req + response)
- counterpartyEmail → counterparty_email (invite)
- passphraseLink → passphrase_link (invite)
- moduleIds → module_ids (split, batch-ready)
- newTitle → new_title (split)
- moduleOrder → module_order (sequence)
- responseContent → response_content (resolve proposal)
- agreementId → agreement_id (create verification)

names. Deploy backend first, then frontend immediately after.

  before appending alpha, preventing broken tint colors.

  Form', so user renames before first save are preserved.

  after 5s) instead of silently failing.

commits.subject: fix(forms): align condition operator behavior and validation with frontend
commits.body:
The previous commit aligned structural types but operator behavior still diverged
from four-ems, risking silent data loss when backend condition evaluation produced
different visibility results than the frontend.

Conditions: equals/not_equals now normalize to trimmed strings and sort arrays
(checkbox groups), contains/not_contains are case-insensitive, greater_than/
less_than use Number() coercion (builder stores values as strings), is_empty/
is_not_empty trim strings and handle booleans/numbers explicitly.

Validation: phone regex matches frontend (no dots/slashes, max 20 chars), date
fields enforce min/max bounds, text regex has ReDoS protection (isSafeRegex +
1000-char cap).

Tags: forms, conditions, validation, security, alignment
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 2
Impact: api
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: field-conditions-alignment
Semver: patch

https://claude.ai/code/session_01JqVFZ316cyaysF7G984H7C
commits.title: align condition operator behavior and validation with frontend

commits.subject: fix(forms): align field types and condition logic with frontend
commits.body:
The frontend (four-ems) and backend had diverged on field types and
condition evaluation. checkbox_group submissions were unvalidated (fell
through to default), condition model used different shapes (fieldId vs
field_id, targetFieldId vs per-field conditions), and type definitions
included backend-only types with no frontend components.

Changes:
- FieldType: removed url/file/scale/multi_select, added checkbox_group/heading/hidden
- Condition: fieldId → field_id, value accepts string[], added in/not_in operators
- ConditionGroup: targetFieldId → target_id + target_type + action (show/hide)
- FormField: added frontend rendering-hint properties (layout, min/max_selections, etc.)
- Validation: checkbox_group with min/max selections, separate rating from number, removed dead cases
- Conditions: per-field conditions, page-level conditions, action-aware evaluation

Tags: forms, validation, conditions, types, alignment
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: api
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: four-ems-forms
Semver: minor

commits.subject: fix(builder): warn user when imported form hasn't been saved
commits.body:
When the import API fails, the form data is passed via router state to
/forms/new. Previously this was silent — no feedback, and the router
state was immediately cleared so a refresh would lose the data.

Now:
- Shows an amber banner: "This imported form hasn't been saved yet.
  Click Create to save it."
- Keeps router state until save succeeds, so refresh preserves the data
- Clears banner and router state after successful save

commits.body:
Frontend (sun-sea-o) has migrated from direct Supabase to the REST API.

2. New endpoints:
   - POST /projects/:id/accept-handshake — counterparty handshake acceptance
   - POST /modules/:id/move — move module up/down (swap positions)

3. Unscoped GET endpoints (optional parent ID params):
   - GET /agreements — optional projectId
   - GET /modules — optional agreementId
   - GET /notes — optional agreementId

   - GET /pdf-verifications — optional agreementId/code

17 new validation tests added. All 89 tests pass. Typecheck clean.

commits.subject: fix(api): correct API contract mismatches found during review
commits.body:
Breaking fixes:
- PUT /proposals/:id: response_content → responseContent (camelCase)
- POST /pdf-verifications: agreement_id → agreementId (camelCase)

Data loss prevention:
- Module intent/summary fields: split into API fields (title, content,
  nudge, expire) + direct Supabase for unsupported fields (intent,
  summary_does, summary_when, summary_consequence)
- Note/proposal sender_tone: create via API then update tone via Supabase
- Both are temporary until tool-till-tees validation is updated

Architecture fix:
- GET endpoints for agreements, modules, notes, proposals, pdf-verifications
  require parent ID params — restored Supabase for global initial fetch
  (hooks use global store + client-side filtering)
- Notifications and signatures work without params — kept on API
- Removed dead resetSequenceSignatures (unused, sent empty body)
- Cleaned up createAgreement to not send server-determined fields

commits.subject: feat(api): migrate data layer from direct Supabase to tool-till-tees API
commits.body:
All 8 data hooks now route mutations through the tool-till-tees REST API
instead of direct Supabase queries. Supabase client retained for auth and
realtime subscriptions only.

New src/api/ layer:
- client.ts: base fetch wrapper with auth headers and error normalization
- Per-resource modules: projects, agreements, modules, proposals, notes,
  notifications, signatures, pdfVerifications

Migrated hooks: useProjects, useAgreements, useModules, useProposals,
useNotes, useNotifications, useSignatureLog, usePdfVerifications.

Migrated AgreementDetailPage.tsx splitAgreement from supabase.rpc to hook.

Remaining Supabase RPC calls (need API endpoints in tool-till-tees):
- acceptHandshake (no standalone endpoint)
- moveModule (API expects full order, not direction)
- fetchVersions bulk query (no bulk versions endpoint)

commits.subject: feat(mobile): redesign app for mobile-first with bottom sheet + bottom nav
commits.body:
Major mobile UX overhaul. On viewports < 1024px, the app now renders a
native-app-like layout: fixed viewport with edge-to-edge canvas, bottom
navigation bar (6 tabs including Export), and a touch-draggable bottom
sheet for all tab content. Desktop layout is preserved unchanged.

Key changes:
- useIsMobile hook for conditional layout rendering at lg breakpoint
- BottomSheet component with 3 snap points (closed/half/full) and touch drag
- MobileNav with 6-tab bottom bar (Presets, Media, Content, Structure, Style, Export)
- Mobile header collapsed to compact bar with overflow menu
- Canvas fills available space edge-to-edge on mobile
- Swipe left/right between pages on mobile canvas
- Platform info strip below canvas opens export sheet on tap
- Larger touch targets: CollapsibleSection headers, range input thumbs
- Body safe-area padding handled per-component on mobile

(added condition, declaration; replaced protection with remedy),
increased max length to 60 chars, relaxed normalization to allow

(summary_does, summary_when, summary_consequence) per module.

counterparty sees them in a summary card above clause content.

commits.subject: fix(aud): race conditions, timer leaks, ineffective memo, partial failures
commits.body:
ResponsesPage: Fix status change race condition — read prevStatus
synchronously before the state setter instead of inside it, preventing
stale captures under concurrent status changes.

ResponsesPage: Use Promise.allSettled for loading submissions + form
definition, so a partial API failure preserves the successful response
instead of discarding both and falling back to sample data.

embedResize: Add disposed flag to guard mutation debounce timer callback
against firing after cleanup, preventing stale DOM reads on unmount.

BuilderCanvas: Pass stable Zustand store functions to SortableFieldCard
instead of inline arrow functions. Handlers are built inside the memoized
component via useCallback, making memo comparison effective during drag.

BuilderPage: Guard title edit against readOnly mode — check store state
before calling updateFormTitle to prevent silent failures.

Tags: bugfix, race-condition, memoization, timer-leak, audit
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: form-builder
Semver: patch

commits.title: race conditions, timer leaks, ineffective memo, partial failures

commits.subject: fix(rev): array comparison bug in conditions, auto-save debounce churn
commits.body:
conditions.ts: normalizeForComparison joined arrays with commas, causing
false matches when values contained commas (e.g. ["a,b","c"] matched
["a","b,c"]). Now uses null-byte separator with sorted element-wise
comparison for equals/not_equals on array values (checkbox groups).

useAutoSave.ts: removed `definition` from useEffect deps — the save
function reads current definition from the store at call-time, so
including it restarted the 2s debounce timer on every keystroke.

Tags: bugfix, conditions, auto-save, review
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: form-builder
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix(audit): timer leaks, race conditions, and double-save protection
commits.body:
Code audit fixes:

- ResponsesPage: Track setTimeout IDs in refs, clean up on unmount to
  prevent setState on dead component. Fix stale closure in handleStatusChange
  by reverting only the specific submission that failed (not the full array),
  preserving concurrent optimistic updates to other submissions.
- useAutoSave: Add isSavingRef guard to prevent concurrent save() calls.
  If a save is in-flight, the next debounce trigger is skipped. The
  finally block resets the flag so the next dirty change triggers a save.
- usePWAInstall: Document why the eslint-disable-next-line is intentional
  (all deps are constants from useState initializers, never change).

Tags: audit, timer-leak, race-condition, auto-save, pwa

commits.subject: fix(review): strengthen edge cases across conditions, validation, embed, and builder
commits.body:
Code review sweep fixes:

- conditions.ts: Replace raw String() coercion with normalizeForComparison()
  that trims whitespace and handles null/undefined consistently. Prevents
  accidental type coercion between number/string field values.
- embedResize.ts: Debounce MutationObserver callback (100ms) to reduce
  excessive postMessage calls when many DOM mutations fire in quick succession.
  Clean up timer on disconnect.
- FieldPropertiesPanel.tsx: Add maxLength=255 to field label input to prevent
  layout-breaking long labels in the builder.
- FormRenderer.tsx: Improve hidden field URL param normalization — now replaces
  all non-alphanumeric chars (not just spaces) with underscores. Also caps
  hidden field values at 10k chars as a safeguard against malicious definitions.
- validation.ts: Show a clear "too long to validate" message when input exceeds
  1000 chars, instead of silently truncating and giving a misleading error.
- TODO.md: Track server-side CSV export as a scalability item.

Tags: review, conditions, validation, embed, builder, security

commits.subject: fix(quality): address code audit findings across 10 files
commits.body:
Anti-patterns fixed:
- App.jsx: Keyboard listener uses useRef for latest values, registered
  once instead of re-registering on every state change (7+ dep churn)
- designStorage.js: Cache IDB connection with auto-reconnect on close,
  eliminates opening a new connection per CRUD operation
- Toast.jsx: Toast ID wraps at MAX_SAFE_INTEGER to prevent overflow
  in long sessions

Latent bugs fixed:
- useFocusTrap.js: try/catch around focus restore on unmount (element
  may have been removed from DOM)
- useHistory.js: deepEqual has MAX_DEPTH=20 guard to prevent stack
  overflow on corrupted/circular state
- ContextBar.jsx: Memoized page state lookups via PageDots component,
  avoids calling getPageState() for every page on every render
- SaveLoadModal.jsx: activeRef guard prevents stale setDesigns() call
  if modal closes while IDB query is in-flight

Resilience improvements:
- ExportButtons.jsx: Multi-platform export catches per-platform errors,
  exports successful ones in ZIP, warns user which platforms failed
- layouts.js: Documented gradient stop magic numbers with empirical
  tuning rationale (anti-banding for canvas export)
- DebugPill.jsx: Clipboard copy uses ClipboardItem Blob API before
  falling back to deprecated execCommand

  same-minute export filename collisions
- App: guard against division by zero in autoScale when platform
  dimensions are 0

  was already correct — IDs compared by array index)

  migrations retry on next app load instead of silently losing data

commits.subject: fix(security,perf): XSS protection, race conditions, memory leaks, and performance
commits.body:
Security: Sanitize marked HTML output with DOMPurify to prevent XSS via
user-supplied text in both structured and freeform text modes.

Race conditions: Use ref for selectedCell in sample image loading so async
callbacks read current value instead of stale closure capture. Add unmount
guards to prevent state updates on unmounted components.

Performance: Replace JSON.stringify comparison in useHistory with recursive
deepEqual that exits at first mismatch. Replace JSON.parse/JSON.stringify
deep clones with structuredClone. Memoize layout preset comparison in
TemplatesTab. Wrap AdCanvas with React.memo.

Memory leaks: Track setTimeout IDs in ContentTab focus handlers and clear
on unmount. Remove orphaned inline SVG filter with random ID generation.

Input validation: Add 10 MB file size limit on image uploads with toast
feedback to prevent memory issues from large base64 strings in undo history.

Tags: security, performance, race-condition, memory-leak, xss, state
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: hardening
Semver: patch

   chain through App.jsx.

commits.body:
Address 11 issues found during deep code review:

- FuelMapWeb: Replace Math.min(...spread) with reduce (crash on empty array)

  (fixes false positive change detection with trailing zeros)

- Station detail: Guard prices[0] access with null check (empty array crash)

- PriceEditor: Remove fuelTypes.length from dep array (anti-pattern)
- Admin layout: Remove router from useEffect dep array (unstable reference)
- useRecentSearches: Validate coordinates in isDuplicate to prevent NaN bypass

All 52 tests pass.

commits.subject: refactor(content): extract shared TextStyleControls, improve freeform UX
commits.body:
Six improvements to the multi-block freeform text implementation:

1. Auto-create first block: FreeformCellEditor creates a block via useEffect
   when cell is empty, so users can type immediately without clicking "Add"
2. Single-block simplicity: hide #N label and move up/down buttons when only
   one block exists — cleaner for the common case
3. Delete confirmation timeout: 3-second fallback timer alongside onBlur, since
   blur fires unreliably on mobile touch devices
4. Focused toolbar: markdown formatting toolbar only renders when the block's
   textarea is focused, reducing repeated chrome with 3+ blocks
5. Shared TextStyleControls: extracted identical style panel (align, color, size,
   bold/italic, spacing, spacer/line) used by both TextElementEditor and
   FreeformBlockEditor — eliminates ~150 lines of duplicated JSX
6. Icon components: GearIcon, CloseIcon, ChevronUpIcon, ChevronDownIcon extracted
   as named components, replacing repeated inline SVG path strings

adjacent section. For row layouts: "To Row N" buttons move a cell to the

commits.subject: fix(content): auto-set visible: true when typing into uninitialized text element
commits.body:
ContentTab's visibility check (visible !== false) treated undefined as
visible, showing the toggle as ON. But AdCanvas checked
cellText[id]?.visible directly, treating undefined as hidden. This meant
typing content into a new element showed the toggle as enabled while the
text didn't actually render on the canvas.

Now the textarea onChange writes visible: true to state when the element
has never had visibility explicitly set (visible === undefined). If the
user manually set visible: false, that choice is respected.

commits.subject: refactor(structure): unify cell selection with global selectedCell
commits.body:
Replaced the parallel structureSelection state system with the same
global selectedCell used by all other tabs. Section context (which
row/column a cell belongs to) is now derived via cellToSection() instead
of stored separately.

What changed:
- Removed internal CellGrid component (~200 lines), structureSelection
  state, lastInternalCell ref, and the sync useEffect
- Now uses MiniCellGrid (same as MediaTab, ContentTab, StyleTab)
- Section editing and cell subdivision editing are both shown based on
  the selected cell's derived section, no toggle needed
- All structural operations (swap, insert, remove, add/remove
  subdivision) simplified — just update global selectedCell directly

Why: The dual-state system caused sync bugs where ContextBar and other
tabs showed a different cell than the Structure tab. The sync logic was
complex (useEffect with ref guards, manual updates in 5 operations) and
still had edge cases. Using a single source of truth eliminates the
entire category of sync bugs.

Tags: state, layout, structure, ux, cell-selection, refactor
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: cell-selection-sync
Semver: minor

commits.title: unify cell selection with global selectedCell

commits.subject: fix(structure): sync global cell selection during layout structure changes
commits.body:
LayoutTab maintained an internal structureSelection that correctly followed
swaps, inserts, and removals — but never updated the global selectedCell
(used by ContextBar, MediaTab, ContentTab, StyleTab, and canvas overlay).
After reordering rows/columns, the global selection pointed to the wrong cell.

Now all five structure-changing operations (swapSections, insertSection,
removeSection, addSubdivision, removeSubdivision) update the global
selectedCell via onSelectCell and sync lastInternalCell.current to prevent
the useEffect sync from re-triggering.

commits.subject: feat(structure): add snap-to-fit button for contained images
commits.body:
Users can now click "Snap to Fit Image" in the Structure tab to
automatically adjust a cell's boundary so a contained image fills
the cell with no empty space and no cropping. The button appears
when a cell has an image set to "contain" fit mode.

The snap direction is determined by comparing the image's aspect
ratio to the cell's: wider images adjust the section boundary
(row height / column width), narrower images adjust the subdivision
boundary (cell width / cell height).

Image natural dimensions are now stored on upload for the calculation.
A fallback async load handles images uploaded before this feature.

commits.subject: fix(tags): suggestions and autocomplete not appearing in edit modal
commits.body:
Two bugs caused the tag input to appear completely dead:

1. ML confidence threshold was 0.5 — zero-shot classification on short
   transaction descriptions (e.g. "Pick n Pay") routinely scores 0.2-0.4
   for correct labels, so every suggestion was silently filtered out.
   Lowered to 0.15 and added debug logging when all suggestions are
   below threshold.

2. Autocomplete only searched tagCache, which is empty for demo
   workspaces and first sessions (tagCache is only populated on save).
   Now merges tagCache with a knownTags prop containing all tags from
   existing transactions in the workspace.

commits.subject: feat(debug): add comprehensive debug logging across all services and components
commits.body:
Previously, only infrastructure code (boot, DB, workers, PWA) had debug logging.
Core business logic — graph CRUD, workspace management, query engine, analysis
algorithms, import/export, similarity detection — was completely silent in the
debug pill. This makes alpha-phase diagnosis difficult on deployed environments.

Added 9 new DebugSource types (workspace, import, export, query, analysis,
similarity, view, canvas, snapshot) with distinct colors. Added logging to:
- workspaceService (CRUD, cascade delete)
- viewService (CRUD)
- importExportService (validation, export)
- mergeService (semantic merge proposal)
- queryEngine (pipeline execution, hop expansion, find connections)
- similarityService (node/arc pair finding)
- analysisService (all 6 algorithms)
- snapshotService (take, clear)
- embeddingService (batch start/end, up-to-date checks)
- tagSuggestionService (suggestion latency)
- GraphCanvas (Cytoscape init, layout execution)
- eventBus (console.error → debugLog for listener errors)

Updated eventBus test to match debugLog change (no longer spies on console.error).

commits.subject: feat(labels): add zero-shot NLI label suggestion system
commits.body:
Implement label suggestions using zero-shot NLI classification
(xtremedistil-l6-h256-zeroshot-v1.1-all-33, ~13MB q8 model) that
suggests relevant labels when creating or editing nodes. The system
uses a separate Web Worker from the embedding engine since it runs
a different model and pipeline type.

Architecture:
- tagSuggestionWorker.ts: Web Worker for NLI classification
- tagSuggestionService.ts: Worker lifecycle with ref-counted disposal
- useTagSuggestions.ts: React hook with acquire/release lifecycle
- LabelSuggestions.tsx: Inline suggestion badges below label inputs

Integration points:
- CreateNodeModal: suggests labels based on title
- DetailPanel (NodeDetail edit mode): suggests based on title + content

Candidate labels sourced from workspace metadata (useGraphMetadata)
plus default fallbacks for empty workspaces. Model download triggered
on first "Suggest Labels" click (lazy, not eager). multi_label: true
for independent scoring. Gated by config.enableLabelSuggestions.

Tags: labels, suggestions, nli, zero-shot, ml, worker, ui

Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low

commits.subject: fix: harden edge cases from codebase audit
commits.body:
1. Import wizard state reset: Clear parsedRows, classifiedGroups, step,
   and error on mount — prevents stale data leaking into second import.

2. CSV parser unclosed quotes: parseCSVRow and splitCSVLines now treat
   unclosed quotes as end-of-field rather than corrupting all subsequent
   field parsing for the rest of the file.

3. Cluster memberIndices bounds check: Filter out-of-bounds indices before
   accessing groups array during embedding-based merge — prevents crash
   if clustering returns unexpected indices.

4. Date disambiguation validation: Skip malformed date samples (missing
   segments, NaN values) instead of crashing on parts[0]/parts[1] access.

5. Forecast prediction bands capped at 90 days: Beyond that, sqrt(horizon)
   scaling makes bands too wide to be actionable. Bands now plateau at
   day 90 rather than growing indefinitely.

Tags: bugfix, import, csv, forecast, robustness

commits.subject: fix: address 10 bugs found in comprehensive codebase review
commits.body:
HIGH:
- embeddingService: worker.onerror now rejects all pending promises
  and emits engine:error (prevents infinite hangs on worker crash)
- embeddingWorker: error responses include id field so service-side
  handler can reject the correct callback; embedBatch early error
  sends batchDone signal
- embeddingService: computeEmbeddingVector uses unique request IDs
  (counter suffix) to prevent callback collision with auto-embedding
- embeddingService: fallback handler for error messages without id

MEDIUM:
- workspaceService: deleteWorkspace now cleans up embeddings and
  arcEmbeddings tables (prevents orphaned data accumulation)
- useGraphData: removed re-throw in async effect, added setLoading(false)
  in catch (prevents unhandled rejection + permanent loading spinner)
- App.tsx: onConfirm wrapper now awaits async callbacks before closing
  dialog (prevents premature dismissal during undo import)
- App.tsx: clearSelection moved inside try block in handleBulkDelete
  (preserves selection on failure so user can retry)
- App.tsx: separate Suspense boundaries per lazy modal (prevents
  one chunk load from unmounting a sibling modal)
- DetailPanel: key includes node.modified to force remount on external
  update (fixes stale useState after bulk edit or import)
- App.tsx: selectedCount passes only selectedNodeIds.size (bulk edit
  only operates on nodes, count shouldn't include arcs)

LOW:
- seedService: replaceDemoData scoped to demo workspace via index
  (prevents full table scan and accidental deletion in other workspaces)
- importExportService: case-insensitive label deduplication in
  buildLabelsFromRaw (prevents "Company" and "company" both surviving)

commits.subject: fix(branch): comprehensive cleanup and hardening across auto-tagging branch
commits.body:
Pattern matching:
- Fix false-positive bug: amountClose alone could match unrelated
  transactions (e.g. "Car purchase" matching "Rent" by amount).
  Now requires description match — amount is supplementary, not standalone.

useTagSuggestions:
- Replace unbounded timeouts array with Set-based tracking that
  self-prunes on fire/cancel — no more stale ID accumulation
- Split pending map into pendingSingle/pendingBatch with proper
  types — eliminates unsafe union casts through resolve callbacks
- Worker error handler clears timeouts on pending entries

TransactionEditModal:
- Add for/id associations on all label-input pairs (a11y)
- Add ARIA combobox role + listbox/option on autocomplete dropdown
- Debounce autocomplete DB query (100ms) to avoid per-keystroke reads
- Add date validation (required, YYYY-MM-DD format)
- Replace ref-based blur timer with plain let (simpler cleanup)

TransactionTable:
- Guard malformed date strings in formatDate (fallback to raw string)

NewImportWizard:
- Remove dead importedCount ref (written but never read)
- Clear error message on forward step transition (step 2→3)

debugLog:
- Fix misleading "circular buffer" comment (it's a capped array)

commits.subject: fix(export): capture PDF at 2x for sharp rendering on phone screens
commits.body:
pixelRatio:1 produced only 72 DPI — visibly soft on 2-3x phone screens.
The previous fix (d6ede26) correctly identified the 2.667:1 non-integer
ratio as the cause of gradient destruction, but overcorrected by reducing
pixel count instead of fixing the ratio. This captures digital formats at
pixelRatio:2 with 1:1 pixel-to-point mapping (page dims = 2× platform
pixels), giving 4× more pixel data without viewer resampling. Print
formats stay at pixelRatio:1 to avoid a 4.17:1 non-integer ratio.

commits.subject: fix(export): use correct physical dimensions for print PDF formats
commits.body:
Print formats (A3/A4/A5) are defined at 150 DPI. Using 1:1 pixel-to-point
mapping made their PDFs physically too large (e.g. A4 was 17.2×24.4in
instead of 8.27×11.69in). Now uses 72/150 conversion for print category
so PDFs print at correct paper sizes (A4=595×842pt, matching ISO standard).

Digital formats (social, web, banner) keep 1:1 mapping to avoid the
viewer resampling that was degrading vignette quality.

commits.subject: fix(export): eliminate PDF viewer resampling that degraded vignette overlays
commits.body:
PDF vignette overlays looked ~30% quality vs 100% in PNG/JPG exports.
Root cause: pixelRatio:2 capture combined with 72/96 pt page conversion
forced PDF viewers to downsample the image. PDF viewers (especially
Chrome's) use cheap bilinear interpolation that destroys smooth gradients.

Fix: Use pixelRatio:1 (matching image exports) with 1:1 pixel-to-point
page dimensions. The embedded image maps directly to PDF page units with
no viewer resampling. Physical print dimensions are slightly off but
pixel quality now matches PNG/JPG exactly — correct tradeoff for digital.

Also simplified captureForPdf to always use PNG (lossless) and removed
unused JPEG quality/pixel budget constants from the investigation phase.

Tags: export, pdf, overlay, quality

commits.subject: fix(merge): consistent normalization + workspace-only node arc resolution
commits.body:
Two bugs fixed in getApprovedItems:

1. Normalization inconsistency: getApprovedItems used inline
   .trim().toLowerCase() while buildMergeProposal used normalizeTitle()
   (which also collapses internal whitespace). Titles like "Alice  Chen"
   would normalize differently between the two functions, causing arc
   endpoint resolution to silently fail.

2. Workspace-only node arc resolution: getApprovedItems only resolved
   arc endpoints against import proposal nodes. Arcs referencing nodes
   that exist in the workspace but weren't part of the import file were
   silently dropped. Now accepts optional existingNodes parameter with
   the same two-layer resolution pattern as buildMergeProposal —
   workspace nodes as fallback, proposal results as authoritative layer.

Added 2 tests:
- Whitespace normalization consistency between functions
- Workspace-only node arc resolution via existingNodes parameter

commits.body:
Both libraries do JPEG DCTDecode passthrough (no re-encoding), but pdf-lib
also handles PNG directly via FlateDecode. Switched to PNG capture mode
(PDF_USE_PNG=true) to eliminate JPEG compression as a quality variable.

Added diagnostic image download in dev mode — when exporting a PDF, the raw
captured image is also saved as a separate file. This isolates whether quality
loss occurs in the html-to-image capture step or the PDF embedding step.

The pdf-lib API is also cleaner and more modern than jsPDF's addImage approach,
which may handle image XObject dimensions differently.

if and only if the user assigned one via cellImages. Any cell can now have

commits.subject: fix(export): fix PDF quality and file size by using JPEG instead of PNG
commits.body:
Root cause: jsPDF handles JPEG and PNG completely differently.
- JPEG: embedded directly as DCT_DECODE stream (no re-encoding)
- PNG: decoded to raw pixels (~23MB for 2160×2700), then re-compressed
  with deflate — producing files 5-10x larger than JPEG while being
  visually identical

Fix: Use JPEG at pixelRatio:2 with 0.92 quality. jsPDF wraps the JPEG
stream directly, giving sharp 2x rendering and small file sizes.
Transparency loss is irrelevant since PDF pages have white backgrounds.

Tags: export, pdf, quality, performance
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 4
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: pdf-export
Semver: patch

commits.subject: refactor: migrate to IndexedDB, fix mobile UX, clean up compat code
commits.body:
Storage: Replace localStorage with IndexedDB for design persistence.
No more 5-10MB size limits — designs with multiple images save reliably.
One-time auto-migration moves existing localStorage designs to IndexedDB.
Save/load/list/delete are now async with loading states and error feedback.

Backward compat cleanup: Text format migration (old global text → per-cell)
now only runs during the one-time localStorage→IndexedDB migration, not
on every design load. Designs stored in IndexedDB are already migrated.

Mobile touch targets: Increased minimum sizes across all interactive elements:
- ColorPicker: neutral swatches 14px → 24px, theme swatches 20px → 28px
- ContextBar: page buttons 20-24px → 28-32px, cell grid min-height 6px → 10px
- MiniCellGrid: cells 12-20px → 14-28px
- ContentTab: visibility/style toggles 24px → 28-32px, size/bold/italic buttons increased
- AlignmentPicker: buttons 20px → 24px
- All interactive elements: added active: states for touch feedback

iOS PWA: Added viewport-fit=cover for notched devices, changed status bar
style from black-translucent to default (prevents invisible text on light
backgrounds), added safe-area-inset padding.

Offline detection: New useOnlineStatus hook with amber banner when offline.
Tells users their work is saved locally but fonts/images may not load.

CSS cleanup: Removed duplicate overscroll-behavior-y from body and #root
(only needed on html). Removed redundant inline style from html tag.
Added min-height: 100dvh for dynamic viewport height on mobile.

useHistory optimization: Replaced full JSON.stringify dedup with smart
comparison that skips base64 image src data. Compares images by ID and
non-src fields only. Eliminates multi-MB serialization on every keystroke.

Tags: indexeddb, mobile, pwa, ux, performance, cleanup

commits.subject: fix(pwa): replace timeout fallback with direct Brave detection
commits.body:
The previous timeout-based approach was fragile — arbitrary 3s delay,
and a workaround rather than a proper fix. Replace with direct Brave
detection: always show manual install instructions on Brave since its
shields commonly block beforeinstallprompt on mobile.

- Add `promptUnreliable` flag for Brave at module level
- Show "How to Install" button immediately on Brave (no timeout)
- Add Brave-specific mobile install instructions with correct
  language ("Tap" not "Click") and shield context note
- Native install button still appears if beforeinstallprompt fires
  (e.g. Brave desktop without shields)

commits.subject: fix(pwa): show manual install instructions when beforeinstallprompt is blocked
commits.body:
Brave browser on Android blocks the beforeinstallprompt event due to
privacy shields, leaving users with no way to install the PWA. The hook
previously only showed manual instructions for non-Chromium browsers
(Safari, Firefox), assuming all Chromium browsers would fire the event.

Add a 3-second timeout fallback: if a Chromium browser hasn't fired
beforeinstallprompt after mount, show manual install instructions as a
fallback. The native prompt still takes priority if it fires.

commits.body:

map, persistence, and database layers.

- useAuth: memoize context value to prevent unnecessary consumer re-renders

  (fuelhunt_fuelhunt_ → fuelhunt_)
- alert.ts: cancelable: false on Android prevents unresolved Promise
  when user taps outside dialog
- database.ts: bulk_update_regulated_price returns number, not void
- bulk_price_update.sql: validate fuel type is regulated before updating
- price_history trigger: set search_path on SECURITY DEFINER function

commits.subject: fix(pwa): detect Brave Mobile via navigator.brave API
commits.body:
Brave Mobile strips "Brave" from the user agent string, causing
detectBrowser() to misidentify it as Chrome. This led to misleading
"Install Prompt" diagnostics. Now checks navigator.brave API first
(synchronous property check), then falls back to UA string matching.

Also updates the Install Prompt diagnostic message — the old message
suggested a timing bug ("may have fired before React mounted") which
was inaccurate since the index.html early-capture script handles that
case. New message explains the actual common causes: engagement
heuristics and browser privacy settings.

commits.subject: fix(pwa): capture beforeinstallprompt before React mounts
commits.body:
The beforeinstallprompt event can fire before the React app mounts and
the usePWAInstall hook registers its listener, causing the "Install as
App" button to never appear. This is a race condition most visible on
fast connections (e.g. Android Chrome) where the browser evaluates the
manifest and fires the event before the deferred module script executes.

Added an inline script in index.html that captures the event on
window.__pwaInstallPrompt synchronously. The usePWAInstall hook now
checks for this early-captured event on mount and consumes it.

Tags: pwa, install, race-condition, android

Impact: user-facing

commits.subject: fix(diagnostics): distinguish browser-blocked from not-deployed in health check
commits.body:
When privacy-focused browsers (Brave Shields, tracker blockers) block
cross-origin cors-mode fetches, the health check threw TypeError and
reported "API not deployed" even though the server was reachable via
the no-cors probe. This gave users a misleading "messaging service is
offline" error when the real issue was browser-level request blocking.

DebugBanner: After both the health check (cors) and reachability probe
(no-cors) complete, if health failed but server is reachable, amend the
"API Deployed" result from fail→warn with a message about browser
privacy settings instead of claiming the API is down.

InterestForm: Add 'browser-blocked' failure cause to diagnoseFailure.
When cors health check fails but no-cors probe succeeds (server is up),
return 'browser-blocked' instead of 'not-deployed'. Show actionable
message suggesting users disable ad/tracker blocking for this site.

Tags: diagnostics, pwa, form, a11y, mobile
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: debug-diagnostics
Semver: patch

commits.body:
Both memos depended on the entire `layout` object, causing unnecessary
recomputation on non-structural changes (alignment, overlays, etc.).

- cellInfoList: [layout] → [layout.structure] (only reads structure)
- cellPositionLabel: [layout, ...] → [layout.type, layout.structure, ...]
  (only reads type and structure)

commits.subject: fix(content): address remaining review issues — bugs, dead code, perf
commits.body:
Five fixes from final code review:

1. getCellPositionLabel: explicit early return for fullbleed layouts
   instead of falling through to the rows/columns loop that would
   return '' anyway — defensive against future fullbleed subdivisions

2. AdCanvas renderGridLayout: replace .filter() per section with
   pre-grouped bySection Map (O(1) lookup). The cellInfo useMemo now
   builds both flat cells array and grouped Map in a single pass.

3. FreeformCellEditor: remove unused `layout` prop (was destructured
   but never referenced in the component body)

4. MarkdownToolbar: pre-compute groupedMarkdownFormats at module level
   instead of calling .filter() inside the render loop on every render

5. AlignDefaultIcon: change from dimmed center-align (identical shape
   to AlignCenterIcon) to staggered lines (left-short, full, right-short)
   so Default and Center are visually distinguishable

Tags: cleanup, perf, ux, code-quality

commits.body:
Fixes 9 issues found during code review:

1. Remove dead `normalizedImageCells` alias — use `imageCells` directly
2. Remove unused `themeColorOptions` export from ColorPicker
3. Remove unused `textAlignOptions` export from AlignmentPicker
4. Fix useMemo dep in MiniCellGrid — depend on `type` + `structure`
   instead of `normalizedStructure` (new object ref every render for
   fullbleed layouts)

   instead of near-identical rows/columns branches

   with per-section .filter()) — O(1) lookup instead of O(n) per section

   picker rename

commits.subject: perf(export): use canvas.toBlob directly instead of fetch(dataUrl)
commits.body:
Previous approach for PNG/JPG: html-to-image → data URL → fetch() →
blob. The fetch(dataUrl) round-trips through the browser's network
stack to convert what is already in-memory base64 data.

New approach for all formats: html-to-image → canvas → canvas.toBlob().
This is what the WebP path already did. Now all three formats use the
same direct conversion, avoiding unnecessary Response object creation
and base64 encoding/decoding overhead.

Also drops the toJpeg import since toCanvas + canvas.toBlob('image/jpeg')
handles JPEG encoding natively.

Tags: export, performance
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 1
Impact: internal
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: export
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix(export): replace window.print PDF with jsPDF direct download
commits.body:
The previous PDF export used window.open + window.print, which was broken
on mobile: opened an about:blank tab, mobile browsers ignored @page size
CSS (defaulting to A4/Letter instead of platform dimensions), and required
navigating a print dialog instead of downloading directly.

Switched to jsPDF which generates the PDF in-memory with exact platform
dimensions and triggers a direct file download. Also renamed button from
"Save as PDF" to "Download as PDF" to match the actual behavior.

Tags: export, pdf, mobile, fix
Complexity: 3
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid

commits.subject: fix(embed): add allow-same-origin to iframe sandbox so charts can fetch data
commits.body:
The repo-tor embed page fetches ./data.json from its own origin on load.
With only sandbox="allow-scripts", iframes get an opaque origin, causing
the fetch to fail silently. The page never receives chart data and stays
stuck on "Loading dashboard."

Adding allow-same-origin restores the iframe's real origin
(repo-tor.vercel.app) so the relative fetch succeeds. Navigation and
form submission remain blocked by the sandbox.

Tags: embed, charts, sandbox, bugfix
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: embed-charts
Semver: patch

commits.subject: feat(pdf): add print-friendly CSS, no-print utility, and page-break rules
commits.body:
Brings PDF/print output in line with the glow-props reference pattern:
three simple pieces that make window.print() produce clean PDFs.

1. @media print rules in index.css:
   - White background, black text for printer-friendly output
   - print-color-adjust for accurate color reproduction
   - Link underlines for visibility on paper
   - Root container background override

2. .no-print utility class applied to:
   - App header, breadcrumbs, skip-to-content link
   - PWA install prompt, update/offline banners
   - PDF action buttons (generate, print, close)
   - Sticky save bar in module editor
   - Toast notifications
   - Mobile PDF hint text

3. .print-avoid-break class on PdfPreview:
   - Agreement header block
   - Each module section (title + content + signatures)
   - Verification code block
   - Prevents content from splitting across page boundaries

Also adds print: Tailwind utilities on PdfPreview text elements
to ensure readable black text regardless of screen theme colors.

commits.subject: feat(ux): comprehensive UX, mobile, and accessibility improvements
commits.body:
Addresses 30 UX issues across touch targets, mobile usability,
accessibility, and general polish for non-technical users.

Touch targets & buttons:
- Increase all button min-heights to 40-48px (WCAG 2.5.5)
- Fix notification bell, move up/down, delete, breadcrumb touch targets
- Add min-h-[44px] to form inputs and selects

Mobile UX:
- Module editor: reduce rows 16→8, add sticky bottom save bar
- Add unsaved changes warning (beforeunload + confirm on back)
- Fix inverted responsive text sizes (text-base sm:text-sm → text-sm)
- Remove autoFocus from modal forms (prevents keyboard pop on mobile)
- Fix notification panel width (use min() for viewport-safe sizing)
- Fix header safe-area-inset-top for notched phones
- Add PDF mobile hint ("best results on desktop")

Accessibility:
- Add focus trap to Modal (Tab/Shift+Tab cycling)
- Add skip-to-content link
- Add prefers-reduced-motion support (disable animations)
- Increase breadcrumb link tap targets with padding + hover states
- Contextual empty state icons (project/agreement/module/notFound)

Performance:
- Add React.lazy code splitting for all route pages
- Suspense fallback with loading spinner

Design polish:
- Relative timestamps in notifications ("3 min ago" vs absolute)
- Larger status badges (px-2.5 py-1 vs px-2 py-0.5)
- Allow title wrapping instead of truncation (mobile-readable)
- Desktop agreement grid (sm:grid-cols-2)
- Reduced empty state padding on mobile (py-8 vs py-12)
- Toast positioned higher with safe-area buffer
- PWA install prompt delayed 30s to let users engage first

Tags: ux, mobile, accessibility, pwa, performance
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: ux-polish

commits.subject: security: comprehensive security hardening from audit findings
commits.body:
Fix multiple vulnerabilities identified in security audit:
- Add shared secret auth (NOTIFICATION_SECRET) to Edge Function
- Remove message content from push notification previews (privacy)
- Replace querySelector with getElementById (DOM selector injection)
- Validate chatId as UUID before notification navigation (open redirect)
- Move OneSignal App ID to EXPO_PUBLIC_ONESIGNAL_APP_ID env var
- Add CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, Referrer-Policy headers
- Split overly permissive chat UPDATE RLS into scoped policies
- Add database CHECK constraint for 15-min edit window (TOCTOU fix)
- Add display name sanitization + database length constraints
- Use crypto.getRandomValues() for chat code generation
- Remove placeholder push tokens (return null instead)
- Clean up verbose Edge Function logging that leaked data

Tags: security, bugfix, privacy, rls, csp, notifications
