commits.subject: fix: address codebase review findings across hooks and components
commits.body:
- useStations.ts: Replace `as unknown[]` cast with typed `Array.isArray()`
  check + runtime validation. Supabase .rpc() doesn't support .returns<T[]>()
  cleanly without generated types, so use explicit type guard instead.
- FuelMapWeb.tsx: Add `| undefined` to getSource() cast and null guard to
  prevent crash when source hasn't loaded yet.
- useAppTheme.ts: Remove `factory` from useThemedStyles useMemo deps — callers
  pass inline functions which defeat memoization on every render.
- usePWAUpdate.ts: Guard updateApp() with ref to prevent duplicate
  controllerchange listeners on rapid clicks.

commits.subject: fix(gestures): smooth swipe-to-reply animation and add bidirectional swipe on web
commits.body:
Replace React state-driven swipe animation with direct DOM manipulation via refs
for consistent 60fps performance. Previously, each touch move triggered setState →
re-render → new CSS transform, causing intermittent jerkiness. Now transforms are
applied directly to DOM elements during the swipe, with CSS transitions only used
for the spring-back animation on release.

Also adds bidirectional swipe support on web (previously right-only) to match
native behavior. Reply icon dynamically positions based on swipe direction.

commits.body:
We were already writing 'installed' events to analytics via trackPWAEvent
but never reading them back. Now getWasPreviouslyInstalled() checks for a
prior install so:
- The diagnostic warning is suppressed when browsing in-tab after install
- The install banner is hidden (canInstall=false) for returning users
- The appinstalled event listener catches installs via browser UI

This fixes the case where isStandalone=false (browser tab) but the app IS
installed — Chrome won't fire beforeinstallprompt, which caused a false
warning and showed the install prompt to someone who already installed.

and consumeEarlyCapturedEvent() to useEffect with SSR-safe initial state.

commits.subject: fix(chat): always auto-scroll to bottom when user sends a message
commits.body:
The auto-scroll logic in onContentSizeChange was gated by a scroll-position
threshold (100px from bottom). Layout changes from the compose bar resetting
or keyboard interactions could push the scroll offset beyond this threshold
after sending, causing the user's own message to not be visible without
manual scrolling.

Root cause: sent messages were treated identically to received messages.
When the user sends a message, they always expect to see it — the near-bottom
check should only apply to incoming messages from other users.

Fix: Track when the user initiates a send via a ref flag. The
onContentSizeChange handler bypasses the scroll-position threshold when
this flag is set, ensuring the user's sent message is always scrolled into
view. The flag is cleared after scrolling.

Applied to both ChatScreen (mobile) and ChatPanel (desktop).

Tags: bugfix, chat, scroll, ux
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: neutral
Epic: chat-experience
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix(dashboard): correct filter fallback, UTC date grouping, and nullish coalescing
commits.body:
Three bugs found during tab-by-tab data usage audit:

1. Filter fallback to unfiltered data — SummaryTab, TimelineTab, ProgressTab
   used `commitsLoaded && filteredCommits.length > 0` which fell through to
   unfiltered summary data when filters excluded all commits. Changed to
   `commitsLoaded` so empty filter results show zeros, not stale totals.

2. || vs ?? in utils.js and DiscoverTab — getAdditions(), getDeletions(),
   getFilesChanged() used || which treats 0 as falsy. DiscoverTab had 9
   instances of `c.stats?.additions || 0`. All changed to ?? operator.

3. Local time vs UTC date grouping — TimelineTab and ProgressTab used
   substring(0, 10/7) for date/month keys (local timezone from ISO string),
   but pre-aggregated keys use UTC. Added getUTCDateKey()/getUTCMonthKey()
   helpers and applied in both tabs.

commits.subject: fix(data): use UTC consistently for all aggregation levels
commits.body:
Daily/monthly aggregation used substring(0,10) on timestamps (local
time) while weekly used new Date().getUTC*() (UTC). This caused 62
commits with non-zero timezone offsets to land in different daily/monthly
buckets vs weekly buckets. Fixed by adding getUTCDateKey/getUTCMonthKey
helpers used consistently across all aggregation levels and month file
grouping.

Also fixed 2 commits with impact:"infra" being silently dropped —
added alias mapping infra→infrastructure in calcImpactBreakdown,
accumulateBucket, contributor aggregation, and calcFilterOptions.

All 18 verification checks pass after re-aggregation.

commits.body:

singleton. Previously, each remount of home.tsx called register() which

Available" banner to reappear after every navigation.

- register() → getRegistration() to avoid re-triggering server checks

commits.subject: fix(security): comprehensive code review audit — 13 fixes across 15 files
commits.body:
Security fixes:
- CSV formula injection: prefix formula-triggering chars with tab (CWE-1236)
- ReDoS: reject nested-quantifier regex patterns, cap input length (CWE-1333)
- Embed postMessage: validate origin, cap max height to prevent UI abuse
- Debug pill: gate behind import.meta.env.DEV to prevent data leak in production
- Embed code: HTML-encode slug to prevent injection in copy-pasted snippets
- Debug report: redact URL query params to avoid leaking tokens/hidden field values

Race condition fixes:
- Auto-save: read definition from store at save-time (not stale closure),
  only clear isDirty if definition unchanged during async save
- ResponsesPage optimistic update: capture pre-update state via functional
  updater to avoid stale closure on concurrent status changes
- All page fetches: add AbortController to prevent state updates after unmount

Memory leak fixes:
- Auto-save: replace unbounded timeout array with single refs per purpose
- Timer cleanup on unmount is now deterministic (2 refs vs N-length array)

Other fixes:
- ID generation: use crypto.randomUUID() instead of Date.now() to prevent
  collisions on rapid operations
- API client: accept optional AbortSignal on all methods
- BuilderPage: add e.returnValue for cross-browser beforeunload compat

Tags: security, race-condition, memory-leak, audit, csv, regex, embed, pwa

Impact: user-facing

commits.body:
Full code review audit addressing quality, correctness, and accessibility
issues across the codebase:

- Rename InterestForm's FormData interface to InterestFormData to avoid
  shadowing the global FormData type
- Add client-side validatePayload call before network requests in
  InterestForm for faster feedback and reduced unnecessary API calls
- Replace fragile text-as-key patterns in About.tsx and TimelineItem.tsx
  with stable index-based keys (static lists that never reorder)

  re-deriving on every render and make empty useEffect deps honest
- Stabilize showManualInstructions with useMemo keyed on isInstalled
- Refactor DebugBanner runDiagnostics to use immutable array updates
  (replaceLast helper) instead of mutable index-based mutations
- Add explicit ReactNode import in Section.tsx instead of relying on
  ambient React namespace
- Replace console.warn in useRepoTorEmbed with debugLog for consistent
  logging through the shared pub/sub store
- Add skip-to-content link and main#main-content for WCAG 2.1 SC 2.4.1
- Wrap footer social links in <nav aria-label="Social links">
- Add 4 new cv-data tests: project fields, education fields, skill
  categories, and project count (46 total, all passing)

commits.subject: refactor: codebase audit — extract event bus, fix leak, remove dead code, harden localStorage
commits.body:
Full codebase audit addressing dead code, hacks, and bad practices:

- Extract shared createEventBus<T>() utility (utils/eventBus.ts), replacing
  duplicated event system boilerplate in graphService, viewService, and
  workspaceService. This also fixes a bug where viewService.emit() was
  missing try/catch around listener callbacks.

- Fix setInterval memory leak in usePWAUpdate — the 60-minute SW update
  check interval had no cleanup path. Store interval ID in ref, clear on
  unmount and on re-registration.

- Remove ~400 lines of dead code:
  - QueryBar.tsx (legacy, replaced by QueryBuilder)
  - useViewData.ts (hook never imported)
  - CSV import utilities (parseCsvString, parseCsvLines, mapCsvToNodeData,
    autoSuggestMappings, ColumnMapping, CsvParseResult)
  - getNextColor() in workspaceService (never called)
  - parseQuery()/QueryFilter in queryEngine (superseded by AST parser)

- Clean up ViewConfiguration type — remove 6 unused fields (defaultQuery,
  layout, sortBy, filters, rendering, timeProperty) and orphaned
  FilterConfig interface.

- Add safeStorage.ts utility with safeGetItem/safeSetItem/safeRemoveItem
  for localStorage in restricted environments (Safari private browsing).
  Applied to useWorkspaces, App.tsx, TutorialModal, seedService.

- Update all affected tests and documentation.

Memory safety:

- FuelMapWeb: ref-based click handler prevents stale closure in map.on('click')

Cleanup:

- Remove stale `modal.tsx` reference from CLAUDE.md architecture

- PriceEditor wrapped in React.memo to avoid re-renders from parent form state

UI fixes:
- station/[id]: track coordinate validity via explicit flag instead of === 0 check
- station/[id]: fix trailing comma when city is empty

commits.subject: fix(quality): comprehensive code audit fixes across codebase
commits.body:
Fixes memory leaks, type safety issues, code duplication, silent
failures, accessibility gaps, and config problems found during audit.

**Critical fixes:**
- useAutoSave: Fix timer leak + wire to real API (was a no-op placeholder)
- usePWAUpdate: Fix setInterval leak (never cleaned up on unmount)
- supabase.ts: Export as `SupabaseClient | null` instead of unsafe cast
- useAuth/api: Add null guards for nullable supabase client

**Shared utilities extracted (eliminate 3-way duplication):**
- lib/clipboard.ts: copyToClipboard with textarea fallback + success bool
- lib/formatters.ts: formatDisplayValue, formatShortDate, formatFullDate

**Error handling improved:**
- ResponsesPage: Revert optimistic updates on failure, show error banner
- validation.ts: Log invalid regex patterns via debugLog instead of silent skip
- ImportFormDialog: File size validation (5 MB max)

**Accessibility:**
- BuilderCanvas: aria-label + title on drag handle
- SubmissionTable: tabIndex, role="button", onKeyDown for keyboard nav

**Performance:**
- FieldPropertiesPanel: useMemo for field lookup
- ConditionBuilder: useMemo for sourceFields

**Type safety:**
- formImport: Type guard instead of `as string` cast for field type validation
- validation: Consistent Set usage for option validation

**Config:**
- vite.config.ts: Maskable icon → 1024x1024 per CLAUDE.md spec
- package.json: Remove broken lint script (no ESLint config exists)

**Code cleanup:**
- conditions.ts: Extract isFieldValueEmpty helper (was repeated 4x)

Tags: audit, quality, memory-leak, type-safety, accessibility, performance

commits.subject: fix(quality): remove dead code, add error handling, fix hook violations
commits.body:
Codebase review identified several issues:
- Dead mock data files (mockAgreements, mockModules, mockProjects) never
  imported after Supabase migration — deleted
- getSession() in AuthContext had no .catch(), causing silent failures on
  network errors — added catch with loading state fallthrough
- Async handlers in AgreementDetailPage called updateAgreement without
  await and without try/catch — errors silently swallowed, toast fired
  before operation completed — now properly awaited with error toasts
- handleGenerateVerification passed unused snapshotContent parameter
  (server-side RPC ignores it) — removed dead parameter from hook type
  and call site
- useCallback called inline in JSX (Rules of Hooks violation) in 3 page
  files — extracted to component body as clearToast
- ErrorBoundary logged stack traces via console.error in production —
  gated behind import.meta.env.DEV
- Modal close button missing type="button" — could cause accidental form
  submission when Modal wraps a form

Tags: cleanup, dead-code, error-handling, hooks, security

commits.subject: feat(pwa): add install prompt with debug logging
commits.body:
Implement the missing PWA install system:
- usePWAInstall hook: consumes early-captured beforeinstallprompt event,
  detects browser type, tracks analytics, logs all state changes to the
  debug system so the debug pill shows install-related diagnostics.
- InstallPrompt banner: shows "Install" for Chromium browsers (native
  prompt), "How to Install" for Safari/Firefox (manual instructions).
  "Not now" dismisses and persists to localStorage.
- InstallInstructionsModal: browser-specific step-by-step install guides
  for Safari iOS, Safari macOS, Firefox Android, and Firefox Desktop.

The index.html already captures beforeinstallprompt early — the hook
consumes that stashed event on mount, with a fallback listener for
first-visit timing where the SW registers after React mounts.

commits.body:
Two bug fixes:

1. PWA "Update Available" banner appeared multiple times because
   updatefound/statechange event listeners were never cleaned up on
   unmount and had no guard against re-triggering. Added
   updateDetectedRef to show banner only once per lifecycle, and
   proper removeEventListener cleanup in the useEffect return.

2. ToneEditModal custom tone TextInput closed the modal on web.
   The TouchableOpacity + onStartShouldSetResponder pattern doesn't
   reliably prevent event propagation on web. Switched to the
   Pressable + e.stopPropagation() pattern already used by
   ReactionPicker.

commits.subject: feat(pwa): add update banner and fix manifest config
commits.body:
The PWA was configured with registerType: 'prompt' but had no UI to
show updates or let users apply them. Users on cached service worker
versions had no way to get new code.

- Add usePWAUpdate hook wrapping virtual:pwa-register/react
- Add UpdateBanner component (fixed bottom banner with "Update" button)
- Add beforeinstallprompt capture script to index.html (prevents losing
  install prompt on repeat Chromium visits)
- Fix manifest icons: separate 'any' and 'maskable' purposes (combined
  "any maskable" causes browsers to pick the wrong icon)
- Add stable manifest id: '/' (prevents Chrome from deriving it from
  start_url)

commits.subject: feat(tones): replace predefined tones with custom tone tags
commits.body:
Users can now type any single word as a tone tag instead of picking from
a fixed list of 25. Six defaults (happy, sad, serious, joking, sarcastic,
sincere) are provided for quick picking. Recent custom tones are stored
locally for re-use. Colors are assigned deterministically from a palette
based on label hash.

Removed server-side tone usage tracking (tone_usage table,
increment_tone_usage function, global_tone_popularity view) and the
deprecated message_replies table. Tone categories and descriptions
removed in favor of a flat, simple selector with custom input.

Tone selection was cluttering the compose bar. Now messages are sent without
tones, and senders add tones after sending via a "tap to add tones" hint
below their message bubble or through the action sheet. This creates a
simpler send flow and makes tone tagging feel more intentional.

- Add "tap to add tones" hint below own messages (within 15-min window)
- Tapping hint opens ToneEditModal directly
- MessageActionSheet shows "Add Tones" or "Change Tones" dynamically
- ToneEditModal title adapts based on existing tones

commits.subject: fix(export): fix Download Current race condition with React re-render
commits.body:
updateExporting(true) triggers a React re-render that overwrites the
manual transform: scale(1) set on the canvas back to scale(previewScale).
By setting the transform AFTER the re-render settles (100ms wait), the
manual override persists through the toPng capture — matching the same
proven pattern used by handleExportMultiple.

Tags: export, bug-fix, timing
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: export
Semver: patch

commits.subject: fix(export): use blob + saveAs for single download instead of link.click()
commits.body:
The detached <a> element with link.click() fails to trigger downloads in
Safari, PWA contexts, and with large data URLs from print-size exports.
Replaced with fetch-to-blob + file-saver's saveAs, matching the reliable
pattern already used by multi-page and multi-platform ZIP exports.

Tags: export, download, bugfix, cross-browser
